items.title: JPMorgan’s Top 3 Stocks to Crush the Market in 2026
items.description: JPMorgan has released its list of 47 top stock picks for 2026 that its analysts believe will crush the market in 2026. They have assigned each stock an overweight rating and given them a one-year price target for 2026. The selections cover a wide range of sectors, including technology, healthcare, financials, energy, and consumer discretionary, ... JPMorgan’s Top 3 Stocks to Crush the Market in 2026

items.title: Realty Income Corporation (O) Downgraded as JPMorgan Reshapes 2026 REIT Outlook
items.description: Realty Income Corporation (NYSE:O) is included among the 13 Highest Paying Monthly Dividend Stocks to Buy. On December 18, JPMorgan downgraded Realty Income Corporation (NYSE:O) to Underweight from Neutral and kept its price target at $61. The move was part of the firm’s broader 2026 outlook for REITs. JPMorgan made two upgrades and seven downgrades […]

items.title: Stock Market Investors Got a Warning From Fed Chair Jerome Powell in 2025. History Says This Will Happen in 2026.
items.author: Trevor Jennewine
items.description: The S&P 500 has surged 18% in 2025 despite tariff-related economic uncertainty, but Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and other policymakers warn that stocks are trading at historically expensive valuations. With a CAPE ratio of 39.4—a level last seen during the dot-com bubble—historical patterns suggest the market could decline 4% by December 2026 and 20% by December 2027. However, strong AI-driven earnings growth and Wall Street's median target of 8,011 for the S&P 500 in December 2026 offer some optimism.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia is highlighted as an example of strong earnings-driven growth, with stock price increasing 14x over five years while earnings increased 20x, demonstrating that AI sector gains are fundamentally supported rather than purely speculative.

items.title: A Year of Market Fear — and Resilience: Breaking Down the VIX in 2025
items.author: Mike Zaccardi, Cfa, Cmt
items.description: 2025 was marked by significant market volatility despite ending with resilience. The VIX spiked dramatically in April (above 60) following trade war rhetoric and policy uncertainty, but the S&P 500 recovered strongly with a 35%+ rally from May onward. Despite macro noise around tariffs, inflation, and Fed policy, the market delivered 15-20% returns with a maximum drawdown of only 19%, rewarding investors who focused on price action over headlines.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CEO Jamie Dimon's 'cockroaches' comment in October sparked renewed concerns about regional bank exposure to bad loans and triggered negative price action in the financial sector.
